title: hieracium dasytrichum subsp. polytrichoides
intro: Hieracium dasytrichum subsp. polytrichoides, also known as Devil's-bit or Mountain Hawk's-beard, is a flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with a long history of traditional use in medicine, particularly in European folk remedies, where it is valued for its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as a natural diuretic. Hieracium dasytrichoides can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties.
